content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 29, 1992


Edward Duddy
City Attorney
3750 University Avenue, Suite 550
Riverside, California  92501

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. I-92-619

Dear Mr. Duddy:

     You have requested advice on behalf of Councilmember Bonnie
Flickinger regarding her duties under the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹  I am writ-
ing to confirm our telephone conversation of December 14, 1992,
wherein we discussed your request for advice.

     As I explained to you, due to the general nature of your
request and the fact that you are not seeking advice regarding a
specific pending governmental decision, we can only give informal
assistance pursuant to the provisions of Regulation 18329.²
I also explained that due to the nature of your questions, we
really cannot give much more assistance than was provided in
Advice Letter No. I-92-409, which we wrote to you on July 23,
1992.

     The facts are the same as those stated in Letter No. I-92-
409.  Ms. Flickinger is a public finance secretary in the bond
division at Best, Best & Krieger.  This firm often provides
representation to persons coming before the city council.  She
would like to know her duty regarding disqualification.


________________
1
     Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2
     Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Section 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento, CA  95812 ● (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-92-619
Page 2


QUESTIONS

     1.  If Ms. Flickinger's employer does not provide her with
information which would disclose relevant representation, what
obligation does she have to ascertain if her employer is
representing a party with a financial interest in a matter coming
before the city council?

     2.  If it is known that Ms. Flickinger's employer is
representing a party in a matter before the city council, but the
employer does not provide information to estimate the material
financial effect, what obligation does Ms. Flickinger have to
ascertain such information?

     3.  If Ms. Flickinger must disqualify, what must she state on
the record?

CONCLUSIONS

     1.  Ms. Flickinger must make a reasonable good faith attempt
to ascertain if a client of Best, Best & Krieger is appearing
before the city council.  Perhaps she could present a copy of the
city council agenda to the firm to ascertain if the firm is
representing a client at an upcoming meeting.

     2.  Pursuant to Section 87100, Ms. Flickinger may not
participate in or use her official position to influence a
governmental decision in which "she knows or has reason to know"
she has a financial interest.  Under Section 87103, she has a
financial interest in a decision if there will be a reasonably
foreseeable material financial effect on her source of income,
Best, Best & Krieger.

     As discussed in Letter No. I-92-409, Ms. Flickinger must make
a reasonable attempt to ascertain if there will be a material
financial effect on the law firm under Regulation 18702.2.  If the
firm is directly involved in a decision, as defined in Regulation
18702.1, she must disqualify herself.

     If the firm will not disclose a dollar amount which will
enable Ms. Flickinger to ascertain materiality, she need not
disqualify herself from participating in the decision if she does
not know or have reason to know that the financial effect on the
firm is material.

     3.  If Ms. Flickinger must disqualify, she must publicly
state for the record that she is disqualifying due to a conflict
of interest which may have a material financial effect on her
employer, Best, Best & Krieger.  She does not need to state any
dollar amounts; however, the name of the firm is not protected by
any privilege.  Regulation 18740 would not apply in this situa-
tion.

Our File No. I-92-619
Page 3


I trust this answers your questions.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Assistant Chief Counsel

By: Jill Stetcher
Counsel, Legal Division


SH:JS:d1
